topic: Operating Decisions and the Accounting System
title: Adjustments and Quality of Earnings: Which account would be affected by an adjusting entry for...
summary: Analyze a financial accounting concept related to Adjustments and Quality of Earnings.
prompt: Which account would be affected by an adjusting entry for accrued wages at the end of the accounting period?

options.text: It has no effect on net income
An adjusting entry for accrued wages increases the Wages Payable account (a

solution: An adjusting entry for accrued wages increases the Wages Payable account (a liability) and the Wages Expense account (an expense). This entry reflects wages that have been incurred but not yet paid to employees by the end of the accounting period, ensuring that both the expenses and liabilities are accurately represented in the financial statements. This is important for presenting a true and fair view of the company's financial position.
